methods.description: This method describes the process of obtaining the initial protein sequence of interest and identifying homologous sequences from a public database using a basic local alignment search tool.

steps.description: Ory S1 Protein (query protein) sequence from Oryza sativa was retrieved from NCBI database [7] .
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve protein sequence from NCBI database

steps.description: Basic local alignment search tool was used to retrieve the homologous sequences by querying against non-redundant database (nr).
steps.searchable_summary: perform BLAST search for homologous sequences against non-redundant database

methods.description: This method involves aligning individual homologous sequences against the query protein sequence to assess their similarity and proximity using a dynamic programming algorithm.

steps.description: Dynamic programming algorithm was used to align individual homologous sequence(s) with the query protein sequence to find the proximity.
steps.searchable_summary: perform pairwise sequence alignment using dynamic programming

steps.description: LALIGN program which implements Huang and Miller algorithm was utilized for this study [8] .
steps.searchable_summary: utilize LALIGN program for pairwise alignment

methods.method_name: Multiple Sequence Alignment and Consensus Sequence Generation
methods.description: This method selects a subset of homologous sequences based on pairwise alignment scores and performs a multiple sequence alignment to identify conserved regions, followed by generating a consensus sequence.

steps.description: Nine close homologous sequences were selected for further study based on pairwise alignment score.
steps.searchable_summary: select top homologous sequences based on pairwise alignment scores

steps.description: ClustalW, a neighbor joining algorithm based tool was used to find the Multiple Sequence Alignment (MSA) [9]
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple sequence alignment using ClustalW

steps.description: and a consensus sequence was prepared based on the MSA.
steps.searchable_summary: generate consensus sequence from multiple sequence alignment

methods.description: This method identifies functional domains and recurring sequence motifs within the protein sequences using specialized bioinformatics tools.

steps.description: Domain positions of the sequences were identified using InterPro, an integrated resource for protein families, domains and sites that combines a number of databases (referred to as member databases) using diverse methodologies and a varying degree of biological information on well-characterized proteins to derive domain positions in the protein sequence [10] .
steps.searchable_summary: identify protein domain positions using InterPro

steps.description: Multiple Em for Motif Elicitation (MEME) was also used to find common motifs found in the MSA [11] .
steps.searchable_summary: find common sequence motifs using MEME

methods.description: This method constructs 3D structural models for the protein sequences based on known template structures using homology modeling techniques.

steps.description: All 20 allergen homolog sequences and the consensus domain sequences were modeled using homology modeling method using same template (1n10A).
steps.searchable_summary: perform protein homology modeling using a specified template
inputs: Protein sequences (20 allergen homologs, consensus domain sequences)

steps.description: MODELLER, a homology modeling tool which implements comparative protein structure modeling by spatial restraints, was utilized for the present study to construct protein models [12,13] .
steps.searchable_summary: utilize MODELLER tool for protein structure modeling

methods.description: This method identifies potential ligand binding pockets within the modeled protein structures and validates their functional significance.

steps.description: CASTp server, which used weighted Delaunay triangulation and the alpha complex by shape measurement of the domain structure was used for the present study to locate the binding pockets [14] .
steps.searchable_summary: locate protein binding pockets using CASTp server

steps.description: This tool measures analytically the area and volume of each pocket and cavity, both in solvent accessible surface (SA, Richards' surface) and molecular surface (MS, Connolly's surface).
steps.searchable_summary: measure area and volume of identified binding pockets

steps.description: The obtained pockets were validated based on their functional significance and its contribution to the essentiality and allergenicity of the organisms.
steps.searchable_summary: validate binding pockets based on functional significance, essentiality, and allergenicity

steps.description: Finally, the target's functionality significance and its role in IgE interactions were validated in the light of literature search.
steps.searchable_summary: validate target protein functionality and IgE interaction role using literature search
